title: THE INFLUENCE OF PRINCIPAL'S PATH-GOAL LEADERSHIP STYLE ON TEACHERS' JOB SATISFACTION IN INDONESIAN PRESCHOOLS
author: Ramadhani Febria Dwi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
ABSTRACT  

 This study aimed to determine the relationship between principal’s path goal leadership and teachers’ job satisfaction in selected Indonesian preschools. Both quantitative and qualitative data were collected to triangulate the data for each research question since mixed method was employed in this study. The population of the study comprised 123 preschool teachers from the Lembar sub-district, West Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia. A total of 92 respondents were randomly selected from this population. The quantitative and qualitative data were collected using a questionnaire which contains both close ended and open-ended questions. The quantitative data were analyzed using correlation and multiple regression. The results of the correlation analysis showed that there was a strong, positive, and highly significant relationship between the path-goal leadership style and teachers’ job satisfaction (r=.731, p<.01). The multiple regression analysis showed that 54% of the variation in teachers job satisfaction can be explained by principal’s path-goal leadership style. in addition, among the four dimensions of path-goal leadership (participative, supportive, directive, goal-oriented), the supportive leadership dimension (r=.335, p <.05) has significantly contributed to teachers’ job satisfaction. Hence, it is suggested that school leaders in Lembar sub-district have to apply supportive type leadership according to the school situation to enhance teachers’ job satisfaction.

keywords: path-goal, leadership style, job satisfaction, principal, teachers, preschools
